title: Migrate a collaborative knowledge base to local Markdown
decisionStatement: Which migration strategy best preserves retrieval, provenance, and daily usability while moving from a collaborative database workspace to a local Markdown vault?
context: A small technical team has documents, linked databases, tags, comments, and embedded files. It wants durable local files and strong search without losing the ability to understand where information came from. The migration can be staged, and the source remains read-only during validation.

inScope: Page, database-property, link, attachment, and provenance mapping
Search, backlinks, metadata queries, and human navigation
A reversible pilot followed by an incremental migration
outOfScope: Recreating the source product's real-time collaboration features
Deleting or modifying the source workspace
Migrating access-control rules as if they were portable
constraints: The canonical destination format must remain readable without a proprietary service
Every migrated item must retain a source identifier and migration timestamp
The strategy must include validation and rollback checkpoints

criteria.label: Retrieval quality
criteria.description: How reliably users can find information through search, links, metadata, and browsing.
criteria.oneAnchor: Known-item and exploratory retrieval are consistently effective

criteria.label: Fidelity and provenance
criteria.description: How well content, relationships, attachments, and source identity survive migration.
criteria.zeroAnchor: Content or origin is materially lost
criteria.oneAnchor: Content and provenance are preserved with explicit exceptions

criteria.label: Daily usability
criteria.description: How easily the team can maintain and navigate the destination after migration.
criteria.zeroAnchor: The destination adds prohibitive maintenance burden
criteria.oneAnchor: Common workflows are simple and well documented

criteria.label: Reversibility
criteria.description: How safely the team can validate, pause, correct, or roll back the migration.
criteria.oneAnchor: Each phase has tested checkpoints and rollback
